experience.summary: Core insurance platform modernization serving millions of users across critical life-event workflows.
achievements: Owned backend architecture for life-event correction and change-agent workflows, impacting millions of insurance policies annually
Led architectural migration from synchronous REST-based services to asynchronous, Kafka-driven event pipelines, improving scalability and reducing peak-traffic latency
Designed event contracts and service boundaries to decouple tightly-coupled legacy systems, enabling independent deployments and safer releases
Orchestrated complex business workflows using Flowable BPMN, eliminating manual processing and reducing operational effort by ~40%
Mentored and guided 6–8 engineers through design reviews, production incidents, and architectural trade-offs, raising overall team quality
Improved observability and on-call reliability using Datadog dashboards and alerts, cutting unresolved incidents by ~50%

impact_metrics.description: Reduced manual processing through BPMN workflows
Improved unresolved incident rate with better observability

experience.summary: Hyve Group – A global events company requiring scalable systems to handle high traffic and complex event management workflows.
achievements: Scaled backend services for a global event-management platform handling thousands of concurrent users during peak events
Designed Kafka-based data pipelines to support real-time updates and reduce data latency across distributed systems
Acted as technical point-of-contact across teams, aligning backend architecture with product and frontend requirements

experience.summary: Worldline – A global payment service provider requiring robust backend systems for handling high transaction volumes and complex payment processes.
achievements: Improved system scalability and throughput for global payments platforms by integrating Kafka queues with core payment microservices, enabling real-time processing of high transaction volumes
Collaborated on building distributed systems in Java/Spring Boot, aligning backend enhancements with Angular-based frontend teams for a seamless and scalable customer experience

experience.summary: rPDB(British Telecom) - A legacy system modernization project aimed at transitioning from outdated technologies to more scalable and maintainable solutions.
achievements: Participated in legacy modernization projects, migrating batch and synchronous processing systems to event-driven architectures using Spring Boot and Kafka, increasing maintainability and performance
Contributed to critical API optimization, resulting in a 30% reduction in response time and improved system reliability
